project: MAU Thesis: Air Quality Spatial Estimation & IoT Placement

phases.title: Harness setup
phases.goal: Set up the repo scaffold, agent instruction chain, and phased research working method.
phases.exit: CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md -> docs/project.md chain in place; working method documented.

epics.title: Harness and working method setup
epics.goal: Repo scaffold, instruction chain, and phased research method in place.
epics.exit: CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, docs/project.md chain established; council POV files updated; working method documented in AGENTS.md.

phases.title: Problem grounding (§1 Introduction)
phases.goal: Establish why the problem is real, name the gap, and state the research claim in general terms. No method names in §1.
phases.exit: §1 drafted with motivation, SMHI network limitations, IoT placement problem, RQs, and scope.

epics.title: §1 Introduction: problem grounding
epics.goal: Establish why the problem is real and state RQ1-RQ3 without committing to methods.
epics.exit: §1 fully drafted in writing/thesis.tex: motivation, SMHI limitations, IoT placement problem, RQ1-RQ3, scope/delimitations, structure paragraph.

phases.title: Literature review (§2 Background)
phases.goal: Survey the field and identify what the literature leaves unresolved, ending with gap statements that motivate the RQs.
phases.exit: §2 drafted; all citations confirmed; no unverified citations remain.

epics.title: §2 Background and Related Work
epics.goal: Survey monitoring, IoT sensing, spatial estimation, and placement literature; end with three named gaps.
epics.exit: §2.1-§2.5 drafted; references added to writing/references.bib; no % VERIFY flags remain.

phases.title: Research design (§3 Methodology)
phases.goal: Argue every technical choice from the Phase 2 literature. Nothing assumed; everything earned.
phases.exit: §3 drafted; all council conditions met; decision record written.

epics.title: §3 Research Methodology
epics.goal: Choose and argue validation protocol, models, decay method, placement approach, and metrics.
epics.exit: §3.1-§3.7 drafted; all ten council conditions met; docs/decisions/phase3-methodology.md complete.

phases.title: Data feasibility audit
phases.goal: Confirm the Phase 3 methodology is executable with the available data.
phases.exit: All five data sources pulled and verified; SLOO buffer set; audit documented.

epics.title: Data feasibility audit
epics.goal: Verify SMHI, CORINE, EU-DEM, and GEOSTAT data are available and sufficient for the chosen methodology.
epics.exit: 34 stations inventoried (13 pass completeness); preliminary 5 km SLOO buffer set; docs/data-audit.md written.

phases.title: Data engineering
phases.goal: Build the unified analytical feature matrix and resolve data quality issues.

epics.title: Feature matrix construction and EDA
epics.goal: Join all five data sources into one daily feature matrix and resolve missing-data handling.
epics.exit: data/processed/feature_matrix_clean.csv (48,283 x 37) produced; §4 (Data and Feature Engineering) written in writing/thesis.tex.

phases.title: Empirical work (RQ1, RQ2)
phases.goal: Answer RQ1 (estimation accuracy) and RQ2 (accuracy-distance decay) under buffered SLOO.
phases.exit: RF/LUR/IDW evaluated; decay curves and thresholds derived; §5-§6 written.

epics.title: Spatial estimation and accuracy-distance analysis
epics.goal: Run buffered SLOO for RF/LUR/IDW per pollutant and fit error-distance decay curves.
epics.exit: Per-station RMSE/MAE/R² computed; decay thresholds set (PM2.5 ~64 km, NO2 ~6 km); §5 and §6 written.

phases.title: Placement artefact (RQ3)
phases.goal: Answer RQ3 with a national IoT sensor placement recommendation grounded in the RQ2 thresholds.
phases.exit: Greedy placement run; coverage evaluated against the DSR criterion; §7 written.

epics.title: IoT sensor placement artefact
epics.goal: Run greedy sequential placement over the national coverage grid and evaluate against the DSR criterion.
epics.exit: 20 ranked sensor locations produced; PM2.5 coverage 10.9% -> 67.2%; §7 written.

phases.title: Thesis synthesis and submission
phases.goal: Complete the remaining thesis sections, resolve formatting and compile issues, and submit the finished thesis.
phases.exit: Full thesis compiles cleanly, is complete end to end, and is submitted by 2026-08-01.

epics.title: Prepare and submit the 60% supervisor draft
epics.goal: A PDF covering Introduction through Results (plus commenced Discussion) reaches the supervisor, formatted to MAU rules.
epics.exit: Supervisor has the PDF and confirms receipt; course management milestone can be marked complete.

tasks.title: Confirm E8.1-T3/T4/T5 (Abstract, Pop Sci Summary, Declaration of AI Usage) and E8.2-T6/T8 (logo, bibliography fix) are done
tasks.note: Abstract, Pop Sci Summary, Declaration, and the bibliography fix are done (2026-07-01); only the logo (E8.2-T6) remains, and it's a human action.

tasks.title: Full clean compile (pdflatex x2 + bibtex + pdflatex) with zero errors and a rendered reference list
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-01: 37 pages, working 21-entry reference list, zero overfull-hbox/undefined-reference warnings. Only remaining compile warning is the missing logo placeholder.

tasks.title: Visual proof pass: margins, fonts, page numbers, banner, captions against writing/thesis-structure.md
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-01: title page, Abstract, Popular Science Summary, and Declaration of AI Usage pages spot-checked; margins/fonts/spacing match thesis-structure.md. Banner will show the real MAU logo once E8.2-T6 lands.

tasks.title: Submit the PDF to the supervisor / course system

epics.title: Complete remaining thesis sections
epics.goal: All remaining sections and appendices drafted in writing/thesis.tex.
epics.exit: §8, §9, Abstract, Popular Science Summary, Declaration of AI Usage, Acknowledgements, and Appendices A-B are no longer TODO stubs.

tasks.title: Write §8 Discussion (RQ answers; comparison with related work; limitations; implications)
tasks.note: Full treatment is for the final submission; a commenced version (RQ answers + benchmark comparison) is a nice-to-have for the 60% draft, not required.

tasks.title: Write §9 Conclusion (summary; contributions; future work)
Write Abstract (~200 words: problem, aim, method, contribution)
tasks.note: Drafted and compiled 2026-07-01; user to review wording.

tasks.title: Write Popular Science Summary (1-2 pages, non-technical)
tasks.note: Drafted and compiled 2026-07-01; fits on one page; user to review wording.

tasks.title: Write Declaration of AI Usage
tasks.note: Drafted 2026-07-01 with broad disclosure, per user's choice; user to review wording.

tasks.title: Write Acknowledgements
tasks.note: Optional; not required for the 60% draft.

tasks.title: Fill Appendix A: Station Inventory and Completeness
tasks.note: Optional for the 60% draft; nice to have.

tasks.title: Fill Appendix B: Model Hyperparameters (RF, LUR, IDW configs)
tasks.note: Optional for the 60% draft; nice to have.

epics.title: Formatting, compile, and submission readiness
epics.goal: Thesis compiles cleanly end to end and is ready for submission.
epics.exit: pdflatex produces the full PDF with no overfull-hbox or undefined-reference warnings, and the thesis is handed in.

tasks.title: Full formatting check: margins, line spacing, figure captions, table titles, reference list
Fix table width overflow in Tables 4-5 (NO2/PM2.5 per-station results)
Restore truncated writing/thesis.tex end-of-file (\end{document} was missing)
tasks.note: Fixed 2026-07-01: file was cut off mid \end{tabular}; ending restored and recompiled clean.

tasks.title: Council review on §8 Discussion and §9 Conclusion
tasks.note: Final submission only, not the 60% draft.

tasks.title: Compile LaTeX end to end (multiple passes + bibtex); resolve all warnings
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-01: pdflatex x2 + bibtex + pdflatex x2, 37 pages, zero overfull-hbox and zero undefined-reference warnings. Only remaining warning is the missing logo (E8.2-T6).

tasks.title: Add MAU logo file (writing/images/logo.jpg)
tasks.note: Blocks a clean title page for the 60% draft too. Last item standing between the current PDF and a fully clean compile.

tasks.title: Submit finished thesis
Switch \bibliographystyle{IEEEtran} to \bibliographystyle{ieeetr} and run a full bibtex pass
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-01: IEEEtran.bst was not available in the build toolchain and bibtex had never been run; switched to ieeetr and confirmed a clean 21-entry numbered reference list renders correctly.
